Hi. Hello there, ma'am. My name is Officer Lina. Can I help you today?

Well, it was just like really strange and it's probably nothing, but I came out here. I was going to sit and read sit and read on my porch. And this man comes walking diagonally. My dog was here diagonally across my yard. He had on a clear plastic bag over his top. He had an umbrella. He had jeans on and he was across the road at a recycling bin. And then he went that way?
Hey, sir. Do you guys call?

He threw it in the recycling bin.
And why are you soaking wet?
We have your stuff. We have your property, your phone and stuff.
All right, 346. I'll get out of here.
Can you put that down, Paul?
He's still on his property. He's still on his property.
What do you think we're going to have to do here?
What do you think we're going to have to do here?
755 there. Oh, you can see him.
720 Flex and then to the water.
He clearly can't take care of himself, right?
He clearly can't care for himself.

Yes. He is out of this world.
Hey, Paul, can you put that stuff down?
I need you to stand up and put it down. And then you need to stand up now. Stand up. Take that off your head, put it on the ground too.
Be careful. You put it down. Now face face against the door. Put your hands behind your back.
Come back. You got any weapons on you, do you?
Are you still employed with the city of Las Cruces?
You're not? Why do you still have a key card?
How much how much drugs did you do last night, man? What what did you use?

You definitely didn't get it from Mexico.
Where'd you get this stuff from?
720 for a sergeant. If you're free to take it.
This is your stuff? Where do you live?
Okay. Why is your address on your ID here in Las Cruces? And it's pretty far from here.
